title: Himalayan Safety Workshop
summary: A focused session on rope systems, avalanche awareness, and cold-weather risk planning.
description: Join the core expedition team for a half-day workshop covering updated safety protocols and field-tested decision frameworks used on high-altitude climbs.
details: Hands-on practice with modern protection setups
Small group coaching with expedition leaders
Take-home checklist for pre-climb planning

title: Alpine Navigation Intensive
summary: A full-day route-finding clinic with GPS workflows and whiteout navigation practice.
description: This field intensive blends digital tools with classic map-and-compass skills to build confidence in complex alpine terrain.
agenda: Pre-dawn route planning and waypoint setup
Debrief with track review and corrections
details: Bring a GPS device or smartphone with offline maps
Ideal for intermediate climbers and guides-in-training
Includes a downloadable navigation checklist

title: Glacier Rescue Drill
summary: Crevasse rescue systems, partner extraction, and team coordination under time pressure.
description: We will run a guided set of rescue drills that mirror real expedition conditions, emphasizing clear roles and calm execution.
details: All technical equipment provided on-site
Wear insulated layers and waterproof gloves
Participants receive a rescue flowchart card

title: Expedition Planning Summit
summary: A strategy day focused on permits, logistics, and team readiness for 2026 climbs.
description: Spend a day with the leadership team building your 2026 expedition roadmap, from permit timing to acclimatization schedules.
details: Includes planning templates and sample schedules
Dedicated Q&A time with lead guides
Recommended for team leaders and trip coordinators

title: Altitude Acclimatization Clinic
summary: A practical clinic on oxygen management, pacing, and altitude nutrition for multi-day climbs.
description: Dial in your acclimatization strategy with a focused review of sleep protocols, hydration targets, and pacing techniques used by high-altitude teams.
details: Includes a personalized acclimatization checklist
Short guided breathing and recovery session
Great for climbers preparing for 5,000m+ peaks

title: Ridge Line Photography Walk
summary: Golden-hour ridge walk focused on composition, light, and storytelling in mountain terrain.
description: Capture dramatic light with guidance from expedition photographers and learn how to build visual narratives for your climbs.
details: Bring a DSLR or smartphone camera
Includes a printable field checklist
Open to beginners and advanced shooters

title: Monsoon Preparation Briefing
summary: Weather windows, hazard mapping, and contingency planning for late-season expeditions.
description: A focused briefing on monsoon-era risk management, route alternatives, and decision checkpoints for high exposure routes.
details: Includes a printable decision framework
Ideal for leaders planning late-season climbs
Q&A with local forecasting partners

title: Expedition Team Leadership Day
summary: A leadership workshop on communication, decision-making, and team care in the field.
description: Build stronger teams through scenario-based leadership practice and communication drills led by veteran guides.
details: Designed for team leads and assistant guides
Includes a leadership playbook PDF
Small group coaching format
